user_message: Can you extract the security posture from this meeting transcript for Woodgrove Labs?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 09:51 EST
Subject: security posture - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with security posture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 11:46 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Woodgrove Labs

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about security posture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-M trap (No native 're-emergence' pattern distinct from dark-outreach in skill set); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-M failure mode (No native 're-emergence' pattern distinct from dark-outreach in skill set)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-M -- No native 're-emergence' pattern distinct from dark-outreach in skill set
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
